sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN THREE WORDS AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.
Questions 11-16: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer. Questions 17 and 18: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C. Questions 19 and 20: Choose TWO letters, A-E.

questions.content: A champion athlete will be in the shop
options: A. on Saturday morning only
B. all day Saturday
C. for the whole weekend

questions.content: The first person to answer 20 quiz questions correctly will win
options: A. gym membership
B. a video
C. a calendar

questions.content: Which TWO pieces of information does the speaker give about the fitness test?
options: A. You need to reserve a place
B. It is free to account holders
C. You get advice on how to improve your health
D. It takes place in a special clinic
E. It is cheaper this month

sections.instruction: Choose the correct letter, A, B or C.

questions.content: One reason why Spiros felt happy about his marketing presentation was that
options: A. he was not nervous
B. his style was good
C. the presentation was the best in his group

questions.content: What surprised Hiroko about the other students’ presentations?
options: A. Their presentations were not interesting
B. They found their presentations stressful
C. They didn’t look at the audience enough

questions.content: After she gave her presentation, Hiroko felt
options: A. delighted
B. dissatisfied
C. embarrassed

questions.content: How does Spiros feel about his performance in tutorials?
options: A. not very happy
B. really pleased
C. fairly confident

questions.content: Why can the other students participate so easily in discussions?
options: A. They are polite to each other
B. They agree to take turns in speaking
C. They know each other well

questions.content: Why is Hiroko feeling more positive about tutorials now?
options: A. She finds the other students’ opinions more interesting
B. She is making more of a contribution
C. The tutor includes her in the discussion

questions.content: To help her understand lectures, Hiroko
options: A. consulted reference materials
B. had extra tutorials with her lecturers
C. borrowed lecture notes from other students

questions.content: What does Spiros think of his reading skills?
options: A. He reads faster than he used to
B. It still takes him a long time to read
C. He tends to struggle with new vocabulary
A. environmental studies
B. health education
C. engineering

questions.content: Hiroko thinks that in the reading classes the students should
options: A. learn more vocabulary
B. read more in their own subject areas
C. develop better reading strategies

sections.instruction: Complete the notes below. Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.
sections.passage_or_context: Mass Strandings of Whales and Dolphins

questions.content:
Mass strandings: situations where groups of whales, dolphins, etc. swim onto the beach and die
Common in areas where the (31) {{BLANK}} can change quickly
Several other theories:
Parasites
 e.g. some parasites can affect marine animals’ (32) {{BLANK}}, which they depend on for navigation
Toxins
 Poisons from (33) {{BLANK}} are commonly consumed by whales
 e.g. Cape Cod (1988) – whales were killed by saxitoxin
Accidental Strandings
 Animals may follow prey ashore, e.g. Thurston (1995)
 Unlikely because the majority of animals were not (34) {{BLANK}} when they stranded
Human Activity
 (35) {{BLANK}} from military tests are linked to some recent strandings
 The Bahamas (2000) stranding was unusual because the whales
 • were all (36) {{BLANK}}
 • were not in a (37) {{BLANK}}
Group Behaviour
 • More strandings in the most (38) {{BLANK}} species of whales
 • 1994 dolphin stranding – only the (39) {{BLANK}} was ill
Further Reading
 Marine Mammals Ashore (Connor) – gives information about stranding (40) {{BLANK}}
